metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Textiles 10 teaches students the art and craft of working with fiber and fabric, including weaving, sewing, dyeing, and textile design. Students explore how textiles are made, the properties of different fibers and fabrics, and techniques for creating functional and artistic textile pieces. They consider the cultural significance of textiles, sustainable practices in the textile industry, and how design choices affect both aesthetics and functionality.
metadata.general_description: Elective course focused on textile design, fiber arts, sewing, and fabric craftsmanship.

big_ideas.statement: Textiles are both functional necessities and artistic expressions that reflect culture, creativity, and craftsmanship.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students learn that textiles serve practical purposes while allowing for aesthetic expression. They explore how different cultures create textiles with distinct patterns, colors, and meanings.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding fiber properties and textile construction enables thoughtful design and skillful creation.
big_ideas.elaboration: Different fibers have unique characteristics that affect how they behave in spinning, dyeing, weaving, and sewing. Students learn to select materials and techniques based on desired outcomes.

big_ideas.statement: The textile industry has significant environmental and social impacts that demand critical awareness and sustainable practice.
big_ideas.elaboration: Students explore how textiles are produced, the resources used, and the human and environmental costs. They learn about sustainable alternatives and ethical practices in textile creation.

big_ideas.statement: Textile design combines technical skill with creativity to create pieces that are both beautiful and functional.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through practice and iteration, students develop confidence in designing and creating textiles that work well and express their vision, whether in traditional or contemporary forms.

i_can_statements: I can sketch and plan textile designs
I can create original patterns and color schemes
I can adapt traditional patterns and techniques
I can consider function and aesthetics in textile design
I can choose fibers and fabrics for specific projects
I can match techniques to design goals
I can experiment with color, texture, and pattern
I can evaluate how choices affect the final product

competencies.raw: Create functional and wearable textile pieces
i_can_statements: I can design pieces that are both beautiful and wearable
I can consider fit, comfort, and durability
I can incorporate decoration and embellishment
I can design with user needs in mind
I can research textile traditions from different cultures
I can appreciate and honor traditional techniques
I can create inspired pieces while respecting origins
I can learn from Indigenous textile knowledge

competencies.raw: Develop foundational fiber and fabric skills
i_can_statements: I can prepare and prepare fibers for use
I can dye, paint, or color textiles
I can work with various fibers and fabrics skillfully
I can care for and finish textile pieces
I can set up and use looms effectively
I can execute basic and advanced weaving patterns
I can work with fiber arts techniques (spinning, macramé, etc.)
I can troubleshoot technique issues
I can sew seams accurately and neatly
I can use sewing equipment safely and effectively
I can follow patterns and construct garments
I can add fasteners, seams finishes, and embellishments
I can complete complex textile projects
I can problem-solve when techniques don't work
I can manage timelines and project requirements
I can refine and improve my work
I can explain fiber properties and how they affect textiles
I can understand how spinning, weaving, and knitting work
I can explain dyeing and color chemistry
I can apply science to textile creation
I can operate sewing machines and textile equipment
I can use digital design tools for textile patterns
I can understand textile production technologies
I can adapt to different equipment and tools

competencies.raw: Design and document textile work digitally
i_can_statements: I can use design software for textile planning
I can photograph and document textile work
I can create patterns and instructions digitally
I can share designs and techniques online
I can source sustainable and ethical fibers
I can use natural dyes and sustainable practices
I can reduce waste in textile creation
I can understand environmental impacts of textiles

areas.name: Fiber and Fabric Fundamentals
topics: Natural and synthetic fibers and their properties
Yarn and thread selection and preparation
